refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing solely on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate an analysis report or data set describing the terrain characteristics of the worksite area independently from other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate safety and operational policy constraints into the terrain analysis
Provide an interface to share the worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area

potential_sources: Integration of safety and operational policies may introduce coupling if tightly bound to terrain modeling
Sharing interface could couple terrain analysis with planning/execution modules if not well abstracted
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently of policy integration and sharing mechanisms, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that safety and operational policies can be integrated as separate DPs or modules without tightly coupling to terrain modeling
Risk that incomplete or inaccurate terrain modeling could impact downstream planning and execution
Assumes that the interface for sharing the model is standardized and does not impose constraints on the terrain model representation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the DP for policy integration and the DP for data sharing to maintain independence and reduce coupling in the overall design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions including terrain modeling for the worksite area.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle to generate the digital terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters related to terrain modeling tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems and possibly updates to the terrain model.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Low containment since the Vehicle focuses on mobility and sensing hardware; terrain modeling logic would diffuse into other modules.
Very low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not data modeling.
Minimal relevance; safety services unlikely to contain terrain modeling functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP because it centralizes data processing and modeling functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from the Vehicle module.
Possible interface complexity with external systems via Network Link that could increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain' by producing a digital representation that enables terrain analysis. This mapping is clear, focused, and verifiable against the acceptance criteria requiring a worksite model/map for planning and execution.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from the Vehicle module may introduce coupling and potential diffusion if data handling is not well encapsulated.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal scattering of the terrain modeling DP. The module encapsulates core processing and data management functions, including terrain modeling, which supports architectural clarity and ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry and tasking inputs and produces the digital terrain model, which can be validated against acceptance criteria such as availability of the worksite model to planning/execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' is clearly defined with a well-scoped boundary encompassing terrain modeling, minimizing ambiguity in implementation responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage the dependency on telemetry data from the Vehicle module to avoid diffusion risks, possibly by defining clear interface contracts and data validation mechanisms.
Review and simplify the Network Link interface to reduce coupling complexity with external systems, ensuring that terrain model updates remain well encapsulated within the NGHE Solution module.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear, focused, and verifiable mappings. The NGHE Solution module shows strong containment and low diffusion, with clear ownership and feasible verification. Although there are some diffusion risks related to telemetry data dependency and network interface complexity, these are manageable through monitoring and interface definition rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and well-contained module is preferable to avoid complexity. Therefore, no further sub-FR derivation is needed at this time.

revise.why: To monitor and manage the dependency on telemetry data from the Vehicle module and simplify the Network Link interface to reduce coupling complexity, ensuring encapsulation within the NGHE Solution module.

assumptions: Telemetry data from the Vehicle module will be timely and accurate with proper interface contracts.
The Network Link interface can be simplified without impacting core functionality.
open_risks: Potential diffusion risk from telemetry data dependency if not well encapsulated.
Coupling complexity via Network Link interface may affect modularity.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the digital terrain model meets acceptance criteria.
Interface contracts and data validation mechanisms for telemetry data.
Design review of Network Link interface simplification.
